verification_excerpt.first_chars:
AREA OF LEARNING AND EXPERIENCE
Health and Well-being
Guidance to help schools and settings develop their own curriculum, enabling learners to develop towards the four purposes.

2. Statements of what
verification_excerpt.last_chars: hey will see how healthy relationships are vital for a healthy body and mind, allowing us to thrive.

scope_requested.notes: Welsh Government / hwb.gov.wales — Curriculum for Wales, Health and Well-being Area of Learning and Experience, Statements of what matters. Session 4a-5 — dispositional-domain source extraction. Reuses the Session 4a-4 Step 9 (Welsh Maths SoW) primitive sequence unchanged; scope differs only by URL — content_root_selector and exclude_selectors are identical. See Step 2 investigation memo docs/diagnostics/2026-04-19-wales-cfw-health-wellbeing-sow-investigation.md and Step 1 pre-flight memo docs/diagnostics/2026-04-19-dispositional-source-preflight.md.

classified_examples.rationale: Meta-curriculum framing describing what the guidance document does. Not content about human capacities, not an enabling skill, not a sustained state. Doesn't fit the three-category taxonomy.
classified_examples.text: Guidance to help schools and settings develop their own curriculum, enabling learners to develop towards the four purposes.

classified_examples.rationale: 'Care for and respect themselves and others' describes a sustained default orientation, not a triggered act. 'Sense of self-worth, overall mood and energy levels' are sustained operating states, not occasion-prompted behaviours.
classified_examples.text: From this understanding, learners can develop positive, informed behaviours that encourage them both to care for and respect themselves and others. These behaviours support learners' sense of self-worth, their overall mood and energy levels.

classified_examples.rationale: Surface form is a propositional claim about a causal relationship (processing affects mental health). Stated as a fact to be understood, not as a skill to deploy or an orientation to hold. Even though the statement could be internalised as sustained awareness, the surface content is propositional.
classified_examples.text: How we process and respond to our experiences affects our mental health and emotional well-being.

classified_examples.rationale: 'Having an awareness of our own feelings and emotions' describes a sustained default orientation, not a discrete act triggered by a specific occasion. Ongoing self-awareness is the paradigm example of Category 3.
classified_examples.text: Having an awareness of our own feelings and emotions is the foundation upon which [empathy can be developed].

classified_examples.rationale: 'Learning how to communicate their feelings' is an enabling skill deployed when the learner has feelings to communicate — occasion-prompted. The aim (a normalised culture) is Category 3, but the skill-development content itself is Category 2.
classified_examples.text: By learning how to communicate their feelings, learners will be better placed to create a culture where talking about mental health and emotional well-being is normalised.

classified_examples.rationale: 'Critical-thinking skills applied to decision-making' is an enabling skill deployed when a decision is being made — occasion-prompted. The skills are not described as a sustained orientation that operates without a decision at hand.
classified_examples.text: Learning and experience in this Area can enable learners to develop the critical-thinking skills necessary to consider their decision-making in terms of possible implications, including risks, for themselves and others.

classified_examples.rationale: Propositional claim stating a fact about career decisions. Neither a skill deployment nor a sustained orientation.
classified_examples.text: A key decision that affects learners for life is around their career pathways.

classified_examples.rationale: Propositional claim about how social influences operate on us. Content to be understood, not a skill or disposition to enact.
classified_examples.text: [Social influences] affect our identity, values, behaviours and health and well-being, and often do so without our being aware of it.

classified_examples.rationale: 'Recognise when relationships are unhealthy' is triggered by encountering an unhealthy relationship — occasion-prompted. 'Seek support' likewise is deployed when safety is at risk. Classic Category 2 enabling-skill cluster.
classified_examples.text: Learners need to recognise when relationships are unhealthy and need to be aware of how to keep safe, and seek support for themselves and others.

classified_examples.rationale: 'See how... are vital' is propositional understanding — knowledge about the relationship between healthy relationships and well-being, not a skill or orientation.
classified_examples.text: As a result, they will see how healthy relationships are vital for a healthy body and mind, allowing us to thrive.

checks_run.name: three_category_content_classification
checks_run.sampling_rule: one line per 500 chars of extracted content; floor 10 ceiling 25; lines that are headings, list markers, all-uppercase labels, section-numbered, or fewer than five words are skipped

details.notes: Informational only — source_archetype does not block session closure. Session prompt §Step 4 specifies Gareth judges 4b sufficiency from the recorded distribution.
